(also **Long Island–Post Pioneers** and formerly the **C.W. Post Pioneers**) were the athletic teams which represented the [C.W. Post Campus of Long Island University](/wiki/LIU_Post "LIU Post") in [Brookville, New York](/wiki/Brookville,_New_York "Brookville, New York"), in [NCAA Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") intercollegiate sports through the 2018–19 school year. The Pioneers most recently competed as members of the [East Coast Conference](/wiki/East_Coast_Conference "East Coast Conference") for most sports; the football team was an affiliate of the [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference"). LIU Post has been a member of the ECC since 1989, when the league was established as the [New York Collegiate Athletic Conference](/wiki/New_York_Collegiate_Athletic_Conference "New York Collegiate Athletic Conference").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former US college athletic program

Athletic teams representing C.W. Post Campus of Long Island University

| [Conference](/wiki/Athletic_conference "Athletic conference") | [East Coast Conference](/wiki/East_Coast_Conference "East Coast Conference") [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference") ([Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference") after merger with [LIU Brooklyn](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds "LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds") in 2019) |

The **LIU Post Pioneers** (also **Long Island–Post Pioneers** and formerly the **C.W. Post Pioneers**) were the athletic teams which represented the [C.W. Post Campus of Long Island University](/wiki/LIU_Post "LIU Post") in [Brookville, New York](/wiki/Brookville,_New_York "Brookville, New York"), in [NCAA Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") intercollegiate sports through the 2018–19 school year. The Pioneers most recently competed as members of the [East Coast Conference](/wiki/East_Coast_Conference "East Coast Conference") for most sports; the football team was an affiliate of the [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference"). LIU Post has been a member of the ECC since 1989, when the league was established as the [New York Collegiate Athletic Conference](/wiki/New_York_Collegiate_Athletic_Conference "New York Collegiate Athletic Conference").

The LIU Post Pioneers passed into history after the 2018–19 school year when LIU merged the Pioneers with the [LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn_Blackbirds "LIU Brooklyn Blackbirds"), the [Division I](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I "NCAA Division I") program of the school's [Brooklyn campus](/wiki/LIU_Brooklyn "LIU Brooklyn"). The LIU program competes as the [LIU Sharks](/wiki/LIU_Sharks "LIU Sharks"), with the new nickname having been selected by polling of alumni and students of the two campuses.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Since LIU Brooklyn was a long-established Division I program, the Division II LIU Post teams for sports that had not been sponsored by LIU Brooklyn immediately moved to Division I without the usual transition period for an institution moving to a different division. Teams for sports sponsored by both campuses were merged, and D-II athletes unable to make the D-I teams were allowed to either continue their athletic scholarships without competing or to be granted waivers that allowed them to transfer to another D-II school without having to sit out a season. The LIU Sharks inherited the [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference") membership of the Brooklyn campus.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Varsity teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Varsity teams")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Men's sports (8)**   * Baseball * Basketball * Cross country * Football * Lacrosse * Soccer * Track and field * Wrestling | **Women's sports (11)**   * Basketball * Cross country * Fencing * Equestrian * Golf * Lacrosse * Soccer * Softball * Swimming * Tennis * Track and field * Volleyball * Rugby |

## Facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Facilities")]

The Pratt Center is also a venue for Nassau County and New York State high school basketball playoff games, both men's and women's, along with the Clark Center at the [State University of New York College at Old Westbury](/wiki/State_University_of_New_York_College_at_Old_Westbury "State University of New York College at Old Westbury").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: History")]

### Classifications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Classifications")]

* 1958–1972: NCAA College Division
* 1973–1974, 1978–1985: NCAA Division II
* 1975–1977, 1986–1992: NCAA Division III
* 1993–2019: NCAA Division II
* 2019–present: NCAA Division I (*after merging with Division I LIU Brooklyn*)

### Football conferences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Football conferences")]

* 1957–1971: College Division Independent
* 1972–1976: [Metropolitan Intercollegiate Conference](/wiki/Metropolitan_Intercollegiate_Conference "Metropolitan Intercollegiate Conference")
* 1977–1984: [Division II Independent](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II_independent_schools "NCAA Division II independent schools")
* 1985–1992: [Liberty Football Conference](/wiki/Liberty_Football_Conference "Liberty Football Conference")
* 1993–1996: [Division II Independent](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II_independent_schools "NCAA Division II independent schools")
* 1997–2000: [Eastern Football Conference](/wiki/Eastern_Football_Conference_(1997%E2%80%932000) "Eastern Football Conference (1997–2000)")
* 2001–2007: [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference")
* 2008–2012: [Pennsylvania State Athletic Conference](/wiki/Pennsylvania_State_Athletic_Conference "Pennsylvania State Athletic Conference")
* 2013–2018: [Northeast-10 Conference](/wiki/Northeast-10_Conference "Northeast-10 Conference")
* 2019–present: [Northeast Conference](/wiki/Northeast_Conference "Northeast Conference") (*after merging with Division I LIU Brooklyn*)

## National championships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: National championships")]

### Team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Team")]

| Association | Division | Sport | Year | Opponent | Score |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [NCAA](/wiki/NCAA "NCAA") | [Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") | [Women's Lacrosse](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II_Women%27s_Lacrosse_Championship "NCAA Division II Women's Lacrosse Championship")[[5]](#cite_note-5) | 2001 | [West Chester](/wiki/West_Chester_Golden_Rams_women%27s_lacrosse "West Chester Golden Rams women's lacrosse") | 13–9 |
| 2007 | 15–7 |
| 2012 | 17–16 |
| 2013 | [Limestone](/wiki/Limestone_Saints_women%27s_lacrosse "Limestone Saints women's lacrosse") | 10–7 |

## Individual sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Individual sports")]

### Baseball

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Baseball")]

In baseball, future Major League Baseball outfielder [Richie Scheinblum](/wiki/Richie_Scheinblum "Richie Scheinblum") batted .415 in 1964, and set the C.W. Post records in career triples (12) and batting average (.395). He was inducted in the college's sports Hall of Fame in 2005.[[6]](#cite_note-autogeneratedp2-6) LIU Post's baseball team competed for many years at the Division I level, as part of the [Diamond Conference](/w/index.php?title=Diamond_Conference&action=edit&redlink=1 "Diamond Conference (page does not exist)") and the [Mid-Continent Conference](/wiki/Summit_League#Former_associate_members "Summit League"), as well as an independent.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The current [LIU Sharks baseball](/wiki/LIU_Sharks_baseball "LIU Sharks baseball") program plays on the former LIU Post Baseball Field on the [Brookville](/wiki/Brookville,_New_York "Brookville, New York") campus.

### Football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Football")]

In 1993, future NFL quarterback [Perry Klein](/wiki/Perry_Klein "Perry Klein") played for the C. W. Post Pioneers, throwing for 38 touchdowns.[[8]](#cite_note-auto7-8)[[9]](#cite_note-scra-9) Klein was named the Division II Player of the Year, after throwing for an [NCAA Division II](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II "NCAA Division II") record 614 yards passing (623 yards total yardage), 35 completions, and seven touchdowns in a single game, and a Division II record 3,757 regular season yards passing and 4,025 regular season yards in total offense, while also setting a school single-season records of most touchdowns.[[10]](#cite_note-auto8-10)[[11]](#cite_note-auto9-11)[[8]](#cite_note-auto7-8)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Lacrosse

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=LIU_Post_Pioneers&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Lacrosse")]

LIU Post won three [NCAA Division II Men's Lacrosse Championships](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II_Men%27s_Lacrosse_Championship "NCAA Division II Men's Lacrosse Championship"). Their first title came in 1996 when they defeated Adelphi 15–10 in the championship and their second came in 2009 when they defeated [Le Moyne](/wiki/Le_Moyne_College "Le Moyne College") 8–7 at [Gillette Stadium](/wiki/Gillette_Stadium "Gillette Stadium") in Foxborough, Mass. The third title came in 2010 when they defeated Le Moyne 14–9 in a rematch of the 2009 title game at [M&T Bank Stadium](/wiki/M%26T_Bank_Stadium "M&T Bank Stadium") in Baltimore.

The LIU Post women's lacrosse team had a perfect, undefeated season and won the [NCAA Division II Women's Lacrosse Championship](/wiki/NCAA_Division_II_Women%27s_Lacrosse_Championship "NCAA Division II Women's Lacrosse Championship") in 2007.
